headline: OpenAI Models, Codex, and Managed Agents Launch on Amazon Bedrock
raw_snippet: AWS and OpenAI expanded their partnership with three new offerings on Amazon Bedrock in limited preview: GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.4 models, Codex coding agent, and Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents powered by OpenAI. Usage can be applied toward existing AWS cloud commitments and inherits enterprise controls including IAM, PrivateLink, guardrails, and CloudTrail logging.

headline: WSJ: OpenAI Misses Revenue and User Growth Targets Ahead of Planned IPO
raw_snippet: The Wall Street Journal reported that OpenAI missed internal targets for both user growth and revenue. The company fell short of its goal of 1 billion weekly active ChatGPT users (reaching 900M by Feb 2026) and missed multiple monthly revenue targets in early 2026, losing ground to Anthropic (which crossed $30B annualized revenue) and Google Gemini. CFO Sarah Friar warned that if revenue growth doesn't accelerate, the company could face difficulty funding future compute agreements.

headline: AI-Adjacent Stocks Plunge on OpenAI Revenue Miss: Oracle -7.7%, CoreWeave -7.4%, SoftBank -10%
raw_snippet: The WSJ report on OpenAI's missed targets triggered a significant selloff in AI infrastructure stocks. Oracle (which signed a $300B five-year partnership with OpenAI) dropped 7.7%, CoreWeave (with an $11.9B infrastructure contract) fell 7.4%, and SoftBank (a 13% OpenAI shareholder) sank nearly 10% in Tokyo trading. The report also revealed ChatGPT's share of generative AI web traffic dropped from 86.7% to 64.5% over the past year.

headline: Musk v. OpenAI Trial Day 2: Musk Testifies He Founded OpenAI as Nonprofit to Counter Google
raw_snippet: On Day 2 of the Musk v. OpenAI trial in Oakland federal court, Elon Musk testified he played a pivotal role in founding OpenAI and would not have contributed resources if the intent was to make a profit. He entered the 2015 founding charter as evidence, which declared OpenAI would seek to create 'open source technology for the public benefit' and was 'not organized for the private gain of any person.'

headline: ChatGPT Adds Model Selection in Composer and Moves Thinking Effort Controls
raw_snippet: ChatGPT added model selection in the composer, allowing users to find and switch models from the same place where they write their prompt. Thinking effort controls were moved into the model picker on web, so when choosing a Thinking or Pro model, users can select thinking effort directly from the model picker. Available on Plus, Pro, and Business plans.

headline: OpenAI Publishes 'Where the Goblins Came From' — Explains RLHF Bug That Spread Across Models
raw_snippet: OpenAI published a technical blog explaining how starting with GPT-5.1, models developed a habit of increasingly mentioning goblins and gremlins in metaphors (usage up 175%). Root cause: the 'Nerdy personality' customization feature gave disproportionate RLHF rewards for creature metaphors, which then spread beyond the Nerdy condition due to reinforcement learning not staying scoped. The Codex CLI now explicitly instructs models never to mention goblins unless directly relevant.

headline: Musk v. OpenAI Trial Day 3: Cross-Examination Gets Heated, Musk Testifies for 5 Hours
raw_snippet: On Day 3 of the Musk v. OpenAI trial, Musk testified for roughly five hours with heated exchanges during cross-examination by OpenAI's lead attorney William Savitt. Musk emphasized he is not opposed to a for-profit subsidiary, but said he does not think it's acceptable for the for-profit arm to become the 'main event.'

headline: Musk v. OpenAI Trial Day 4: Brockman Arrives to Testify, Musk Concludes Testimony
raw_snippet: On Day 4 of the trial, Elon Musk concluded his testimony and lawyers sparred over a second witness. OpenAI President Greg Brockman arrived at the U.S. District Court in Oakland ahead of his expected testimony. The jury would not reconvene until the following Monday.

headline: OpenAI GPT OSS Models Available on Amazon Bedrock in AWS GovCloud (US)
raw_snippet: OpenAI's open-weight GPT OSS models (120B and 20B) are now available on Amazon Bedrock in AWS GovCloud (US) regions, powered by Mantle, a new distributed inference engine. These fully open models with open weights, datasets, and recipes are now accessible to government customers through AWS's secure government cloud environment.

headline: OpenAI Introduces Advanced Account Security with Passkeys and YubiKey Partnership
raw_snippet: OpenAI launched Advanced Account Security, an opt-in feature for ChatGPT accounts that requires passkeys or security keys, disables password/SMS/email sign-in, adds recovery keys, shortens sessions, and automatically excludes conversations from training data. Recommended for journalists, researchers, and political dissidents. OpenAI partnered with Yubico for preferred pricing on customized YubiKey bundles.

headline: OpenAI Publishes Technical Blog on Delivering Low-Latency Voice AI at Scale
raw_snippet: OpenAI engineers Yi Zhang and William McDonald published a deep technical blog on how OpenAI rebuilt its WebRTC stack to power real-time voice AI. They describe a split relay plus transceiver architecture that preserves standard WebRTC behavior for clients while changing how packets are routed inside OpenAI's infrastructure to achieve low latency at global scale.

headline: OpenAI Finalizes $10B Joint Venture 'The Deployment Company' with PE Firms
raw_snippet: OpenAI finalized a $10 billion joint venture called 'The Deployment Company' with 19 PE investors including TPG, Brookfield, Advent, Bain Capital, SoftBank, and Dragoneer. OpenAI commits up to $1.5B ($500M equity at close + $1B option). The venture embeds OpenAI engineers directly inside client organizations (similar to Palantir's forward-deployed model) across healthcare, logistics, manufacturing, and financial services. OpenAI retains control through super-voting shares with a 17.5% guaranteed annual return for investors over five years.

headline: Musk Sought Settlement with OpenAI Two Days Before Trial, Filed Ominous Texts to Brockman
raw_snippet: A court filing revealed that Elon Musk sent a message to OpenAI President Greg Brockman on April 25 'to gauge interest in settlement.' When Brockman suggested both sides drop their claims, Musk responded: 'By the end of this week, you and Sam will be the most hated men in America.' OpenAI claims these texts show Musk's motivations are personal, not about protecting the nonprofit mission.

headline: OpenAI President Greg Brockman Discloses Nearly $30 Billion Stake at Trial
raw_snippet: OpenAI co-founder and President Greg Brockman disclosed during trial testimony that his stake in the firm is worth nearly $30 billion. When Musk's lawyer asked 'You just happen to be $30 billion richer?', Brockman responded 'Compensation was certainly secondary to the mission.' He testified the stake was granted in 2018, years before ChatGPT's release, by the OpenAI board without his participation in the vote.

headline: OpenAI Sales Leader James Dyett Departs for Operator in Residence Role at Thrive Capital
raw_snippet: OpenAI sales leader James Dyett announced he is leaving to become Operator in Residence at Thrive Capital, one of OpenAI's longterm backers whose founder Joshua Kushner has a close relationship with CEO Sam Altman. Dyett joined OpenAI in 2023 and led enterprise sales and API sales during a period of explosive growth following ChatGPT's launch.

headline: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar Pushes to Delay IPO to 2027; Altman Wants 2026 Listing
raw_snippet: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar is advocating to delay the company's IPO to 2027, citing $600 billion in accumulated infrastructure spending commitments and missed growth targets that could strain the company's finances when presenting to public investors. CEO Sam Altman is pushing for a Q4 2026 listing targeting a $1 trillion valuation. A 2027 listing would give time to close the gap between infrastructure commitments and revenue, satisfy reporting requirements, and resolve the Musk litigation.

headline: Microsoft-OpenAI Partnership Restructured: End of Azure Exclusivity, AGI Clause Removed
raw_snippet: OpenAI and Microsoft announced an amended partnership agreement on April 27 that ends Azure exclusivity (OpenAI can now serve products across any cloud provider), removes the AGI clause entirely, restructures revenue sharing (Microsoft no longer pays revenue share to OpenAI; OpenAI's payments to Microsoft continue through 2030 with a cap), and extends Microsoft's license to OpenAI IP through 2032. Microsoft remains primary cloud partner with products shipping first on Azure. Market reaction and implementation news dominated the following week.

headline: OpenAI Responds to Revenue Miss: 'Firing on All Cylinders'
raw_snippet: OpenAI pushed back on the WSJ revenue miss report, calling it 'ridiculous' and stating 'We are totally aligned on buying as much compute as we can and working hard on it together every day.' The company said it is 'firing on all cylinders' despite the market panic. OpenAI's annualized revenue stands at approximately $25 billion, while Anthropic has crossed $30 billion, representing a reversal of their previous positions.

headline: Musk v. Altman Trial Opens in Oakland: Musk Testifies OpenAI 'Stole a Charity'
raw_snippet: The Musk v. Altman trial opened April 28 in federal court in Oakland before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers with a nine-person jury. Musk alleges OpenAI, Altman and Brockman breached promises to keep the AI lab a nonprofit, seeking $150 billion in damages and the ouster of leadership. Musk testified over three days that he donated $38 million under the premise the organization would prioritize safe AI development.

headline: Musk Admits Under Oath That xAI Distilled OpenAI Models to Train Grok
raw_snippet: During cross-examination on April 30, Elon Musk acknowledged it is 'partly' true that xAI used OpenAI models to train its own Grok AI through a process known as distillation. Musk downplayed the admission, calling it 'standard practice,' but the revelation undercuts his claim of being harmed by OpenAI's for-profit pivot while his own company benefited from their technology.

headline: OpenAI and Microsoft End Exclusive Cloud Partnership; AGI Clause Eliminated
raw_snippet: Microsoft and OpenAI formally ended their exclusive cloud partnership on April 27, with the new terms taking effect April 28. Microsoft's license to OpenAI IP becomes non-exclusive (through 2032), the 'AGI clause' that would have terminated the license is gone, and OpenAI can now sell products on any cloud. Microsoft retains 20% revenue share through 2030, subject to an undisclosed cap.

headline: OpenAI Models Go Live on Amazon Bedrock Within 24 Hours of Microsoft Exclusivity Ending
raw_snippet: Within 24 hours of the Microsoft exclusivity ending, AWS launched OpenAI's GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, Codex coding agent, and a new Bedrock Managed Agents category in limited preview. The launch is part of Amazon's up-to-$50 billion OpenAI investment announced in February, which includes OpenAI expanding its AWS commitment by $100 billion over eight years.

headline: Brockman Testifies OpenAI Will Spend $50 Billion on Computing in 2026
raw_snippet: OpenAI President Greg Brockman testified on the witness stand that the company expects to spend $50 billion on computing power in 2026 to support its AI software, up from roughly $30 million in 2017. The disclosure came during the Musk v. Altman trial and reflects the extraordinary capital intensity of frontier AI development. OpenAI has separately pledged ~$600 billion in spending through 2030.

headline: Brockman Discloses Nearly $30 Billion OpenAI Stake and Financial Ties to Altman
raw_snippet: During trial testimony, Greg Brockman disclosed that his OpenAI equity stake is worth nearly $30 billion. He also revealed Altman granted him a stake in Altman's family office in 2017 worth $10 million at the time, plus holdings in Altman-backed companies Cerebras and Helion Energy. Musk's lawyers argue these ties compromised Brockman's independence as a fiduciary.

headline: Brockman Testifies Musk Had OpenAI Employees Do Secret Work for Tesla Autopilot
raw_snippet: Greg Brockman testified that Musk enlisted several OpenAI employees to do months of unpaid work for Tesla in 2017, helping overhaul the Autopilot self-driving team. Brockman said this was done without full organizational awareness, rebutting Musk's framing of selfless commitment to OpenAI's mission. Brockman also testified Musk wanted $80 billion from OpenAI to finance Mars colonization.

headline: OpenAI Launches Advanced Account Security With Yubico Partnership for High-Risk Users
raw_snippet: OpenAI introduced Advanced Account Security, an opt-in mode that replaces password-based sign-in with passkeys or physical security keys (via Yubico partnership at $68 for two keys). The feature disables email/SMS recovery, shortens sessions, sends login alerts, and opts users out of model training. Targeted at journalists, researchers, and officials; mandatory for Trusted Access for Cyber users from June 1.

headline: OpenAI Rolls Out GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT Default Model With Major Accuracy Gains
raw_snippet: OpenAI replaced its ChatGPT default model with GPT-5.5 Instant, succeeding GPT-5.3 Instant. Internal testing shows 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims on high-stakes medical, legal, and financial prompts, and 37.3% fewer inaccurate claims on previously flagged conversations. Responses use ~30% fewer words and cut back on emoji usage. Available to all ChatGPT users and via the API.

headline: Washington Post: Musk's Trial Against OpenAI Centers on Billionaire Personality, Not AI Policy
raw_snippet: The Washington Post's trial analysis notes that week one was dominated by Musk's combative testimony and personality rather than substantive AI governance questions. Musk clashed repeatedly with OpenAI lead counsel William Savitt, accusing him of lying and designing questions 'to trick' him. The judge's advisory-only jury structure means the final ruling rests with Judge Gonzalez Rogers, expected by mid-May.

headline: Anthropic Launches Rival PE Joint Venture With Blackstone, Goldman Sachs in Response to OpenAI
raw_snippet: Within minutes of OpenAI announcing The Deployment Company, rival Anthropic announced its own PE-backed joint venture with Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, and Goldman Sachs. The parallel launches signal a new competitive front in enterprise AI where frontier model makers embed engineers inside portfolio companies, following a forward-deployed model similar to Palantir.

headline: Fortune Analysis: Musk-OpenAI Trial Producing 'More Heat Than Light' on AI Control Questions
raw_snippet: Fortune's legal analysis argues the trial has so far failed to advance the public-interest question of who should control powerful AI systems. Instead, testimony has centered on interpersonal dynamics among billionaires. Judge Gonzalez Rogers split the trial into a liability phase (expected to conclude by May 21) and a remedy phase if liability is found.

headline: OpenAI Signs Classified AI Deal with Pentagon for 'Any Lawful Operational Use'
raw_snippet: The U.S. Department of Defense signed deals with seven AI companies including OpenAI to use their technology on classified networks. OpenAI agreed to let the Pentagon use its AI software for any 'lawful operational use.' The announcement comes after Anthropic declined to grant similar broad license and was banned from Defense Department work.

headline: OpenAI Plans Massive Expansion of Cheaper ChatGPT Go Tier, Expects 112M Subscribers
raw_snippet: OpenAI forecasts that subscribers to ChatGPT Go ($8/month in US, ~$5/month elsewhere) will surge 36x to 112 million this year, while ChatGPT Plus subscribers are projected to fall 80% to about 9 million. The company expects to offset revenue decline with advertising shown to ChatGPT Go users, projecting ad revenue per user to rise to $59 by 2030.

headline: Musk v. Altman Trial Begins; Musk Testifies OpenAI 'Looting' a Charity
raw_snippet: The Musk v. Altman trial officially began on April 28. Elon Musk testified that OpenAI's pivot from a charity to a for-profit business is wrong and sets a concerning precedent. Musk claims the founders breached a charitable trust and unjustly enriched themselves. OpenAI's attorneys counter that the lawsuit is primarily an attempt to undermine a competitor to Musk's xAI.

headline: Musk Messaged OpenAI President Brockman About Settlement Before Trial
raw_snippet: Elon Musk messaged OpenAI president Greg Brockman to gauge interest in a possible settlement two days before trial began. Brockman responded that both sides could drop their legal claims. Musk replied: 'By the end of this week, you and Sam will be the most hated men in America. If you insist, so it will be.' OpenAI's lawyers filed the exchange with the court.

headline: OpenAI-Linked Stocks Slump on Report Company Missed Key Internal Targets
raw_snippet: US stocks slipped from record highs on a slump in OpenAI-linked stocks following a Bloomberg report that the company missed key internal targets. The sell-off came ahead of crucial Big Tech earnings and a Fed rate decision.

headline: Microsoft AI Revenue Tops $9.25B Quarterly, 123% YoY Growth; OpenAI a Key Azure Customer
raw_snippet: Microsoft disclosed for the first time that its total AI revenue exceeded $9.25 billion in quarterly revenue, 123% higher year-over-year. This includes sales of specialized AI servers on Azure to OpenAI and other AI firms. Microsoft did not specify what percent of its $627B revenue backlog came from OpenAI, after saying last quarter that the startup accounted for 45%.

headline: S&P 500 Considers Rule Change That Could Fast-Track OpenAI IPO Index Entry
raw_snippet: S&P Dow Jones Indices is consulting on whether to let large stocks into indexes after just 6 months post-IPO (down from 12 months) and exempt mega-cap companies from profitability requirements. The rule change could boost demand for eventual IPO shares of OpenAI, Anthropic and SpaceX. Consultation period open until May 28.

headline: How ChatGPT Serves Ads: Technical Details of OpenAI's New Ad Platform Revealed
raw_snippet: OpenAI's ad platform has two halves: the ChatGPT back end injects structured objects into conversations while the model is responding, and on the merchant side a tracking SDK reports product views back to OpenAI. The two parts are tied together by Fernet-encrypted click tokens enabling full attribution.

headline: Microsoft Shifts to Usage-Based Pricing for AI Products Powered by OpenAI
raw_snippet: Microsoft CEO Nadella said any per-user business will become 'per-user and usage' as increased AI usage drags down cloud margins. GitHub Copilot (which relies in part on models powering OpenAI products) now charges usage-based fees. Microsoft's Azure gross profit margin fell 5 percentage points to 56% year-over-year partly due to AI usage growth.
